THE MEANING BEHIND OUR NAME

Sanad is an Arabic word that represents support, trust, and reliable backing. Insurance exists for the exact same reason: to stand beside people when they need protection most.

The “e” in eSanad represents electronic access, technology, and modern digital innovation.

Together, eSanad means digital support you can rely on.

Support

In Arabic, سند (Sanad) means a pillar of support — someone who stands behind you no matter what. That is what insurance is meant to be.

Peace of Mind

Sanad is the comfort of knowing someone has your back. Every policy we issue is a promise: when life doesn't go as planned, you won't face it alone.

A Document of Trust

In legal and financial Arabic, a "sanad" is also a bond or deed — a formal commitment. Your policy with us is that commitment, honoured in full.

Sanad — Because someone should always have your back.

OUR APPROACH
Transparent by Design. Customer-First by Nature.

Everything we do follows four principles that we've never compromised on since day one.

"We earn your trust one policy at a time."

01

We Listen Before We Recommend

Unlike traditional brokers who push the highest-margin product, eSanad's advisors are trained to understand your needs first. Your life situation, your budget, your risks — then and only then, we recommend.

02

We Compare, Not Sell

eSanad is not an insurance company. We don't create products — we find the best existing products for you. That independence means we work for you, not for any single insurer.

03

We Stay With You After Purchase

Your relationship with eSanad doesn't end when you buy a policy. We send renewal reminders, annual coverage reviews, and claims support — because that's what a real broker does.

04

We Never Stop Improving

Every product update, every AI feature, every new insurer partnership is driven by one question: does this make our customers' lives better? If yes — we build it.

Why Buy Motor Insurance from eSanad?

Looking for fast, affordable car insurance in Dubai, Abu Dhabi, or anywhere in the UAE? eSanad makes it easy. Instantly compare third-party liability and comprehensive car insurance plans from top UAE insurance companies, with instant policy issuance, 100% licensed coverage, and no hidden fees, all in just a few clicks.

What Is Car Insurance in the UAE?

Car insurance -also called motor or auto insurance- provides essential financial protection on UAE roads. If your vehicle is damaged, stolen, or involved in an accident that harms others, your insurance helps cover the costs including repairs, medical expenses, and legal liabilities. In the UAE, having car insurance isn't optional, it's the law. Whether you choose third-party liability or comprehensive coverage, the right policy keeps you legally compliant and financially secure every time you drive.

Why Do You Need Car Insurance in UAE?
Protects you financially against accidents, damages, and third-party liabilities.
Ensures peace of mind while driving in busy cities like Dubai and Abu Dhabi.
Covers unexpected risks like fire, theft, and natural disasters.
It is mandatory by UAE law to drive.
Benefits & Reasons to Buy
Avoid legal fines and penalties.
Mandatory for vehicle registration and renewal.
Access to 24/7 customer support.
Helps you comply with RTA and traffic laws.
Why do you need Car Insurance in UAE?
Car insurance in the UAE is more than a requirement, it's your legal and financial safeguard. By law, authorities require every vehicle to be insured to register or renew its license.
But beyond compliance, car insurance protects you when you need it most
Accident Protection:

Covers repair costs or replacement of your vehicle.

Third-Party Liability

Ensures you're covered if you injure others or damage their property.

Unexpected Risks:

Fire, theft, and natural disasters are included in most comprehensive policies.

Peace of Mind:

Stay confident on the road, knowing you're fully protected and compliant.

With eSanad, you get more than the minimum. We bring you transparent, digital, fast, and designed around your needs.

Types of Car Insurance in UAE

Comprehensive Car Insurance

Covers both your car and third-party damages.
Protection against accidents, fire, theft, and natural disasters.
Ideal for new or high-value cars.

Third-Party Liability Insurance

Mandatory by UAE law.
Covers damages to other vehicles, property, or people.
Affordable option for budget-conscious drivers.

Commercial Vehicle Insurance

Designed for fleets, pickups, vans, and buses.
Protects against accidents, theft, and third-party liability.
Flexible add-ons for business needs.

Classic Car Insurance

Special coverage for vintage and collectible vehicles.
Tailored protection considering car value and limited usage.

Electric Vehicle (EV) Insurance

Custom plan for electric cars.
Covers battery, charging equipment, and unique EV risks.

Who is Eligible for Car Insurance in UAE?

Car insurance is available across all Emirates, including Abu Dhabi, Dubai, Sharjah, Ajman, Umm Al Quwain, Ras Al Khaimah, and Fujairah.

UAE Nationals – Citizens

with valid Emirates ID and driving license.

Expatriates/Residents

Foreign nationals with residence visa, Emirates ID.

Companies & Businesses

For fleets, rentals, pickups, vans, and buses.

New Drivers

Subject to license validity and insurer's policy.

Experienced Drivers

No-claim history may enjoy special discounts.

eSanad provides coverage options tailored for both private and commercial vehicles across the UAE, ensuring convenience no matter where you are located.

How to Buy eSanad Car Insurance (Step-by-Step)

Before you start (quick checklist): Emirates ID, UAE driving license, Mulkiya (vehicle registration), previous policy/No-Claim Certificate (if any).

Enter your car details

Enter your plate number or Mulkiya details. Our system auto-fills your car make, model, and year instantly.

Compare quotes

View real-time quotes from 35+ UAE insurers side by side. Filter by price, agency/non-agency repair, deductible, and add-ons.

Select your plan

Choose comprehensive or third-party cover. Customize with optional add-ons: roadside assistance, GCC cover, personal accident, windshield, and more.

Upload your documents

Securely upload your Emirates ID, UAE driving license, and Mulkiya (vehicle registration card). Takes under 2 minutes.

Pay securely online

Pay via credit/debit card or digital wallet. Your payment is encrypted and processed instantly.

Receive your policy instantly

Your e-policy is emailed to you immediately. Insurance data is automatically synced with RTA systems for seamless registration renewal.

Fines & Penalties in the UAE

Driving without valid insurance can lead to strict fines, penalties, and legal action in the UAE.

Driving Without Insurance

Fine: AED 500
4 Black Points on driving license
Vehicle impounded for up to 7 days

Fraudulent Insurance Claims

Hefty fines
Cancellation of policy
Possible legal action
Expired Insurance Policy
Renewal delays may lead to fines during traffic checks.
Vehicle may not be registered until valid insurance is provided.
Driving a Vehicle Not Covered by Insurance
If caught driving an uninsured or unregistered vehicle, additional penalties may apply, including extended impoundment.

Regional Enforcement

Abu Dhabi and Northern Emirates strictly monitor insurance compliance during renewals, traffic stops, and police checks.

What's Covered vs. Not Covered
Understanding your car insurance coverage helps you make informed decisions and avoid surprises during claims.

What Car Insurance Usually Covers?

Accidents, fire, and theft protection.

Natural disasters such as floods or storms.

Third-party liability for damages to others.

Medical expenses for drivers and passengers (depending on policy).

What Car Insurance Doesn't Cover?

Routine wear and tear or mechanical breakdowns.

Driving violations like driving without a license or under the influence.

Unapproved modifications or racing use.

Coverage outside the UAE, unless specifically included in your plan.

Third-party cover may be enough for older cars

Coverage type and add-ons

Compare quotes before deciding

Why This Matters in the UAE

The Schengen Visa Code demands minimum EUR 30,000 medical cover, valid across all 27 Schengen states, with repatriation and emergency hospitalisation. Every eSanad travel policy exceeds this. A 60 second UAE Pass checkout delivers an embassy-compliant PDF certificate in English and Arabic, accepted by VFS, BLS, TLS and all UK, USA, Canada, Australia and GCC consulates.

Beyond visa compliance, travel insurance protects against emergency medical bills abroad, trip cancellation, baggage loss, missed connections, pandemic medical and pregnancy complications. Annual multi-trip plans are often cheaper than two single trips per year.

eSanad compares AIG, GIG Gulf, Orient, Orient Takaful, SALAMA and Cigna on one screen.

Practical Broker Tips

BROKER TIP 1

Buy before visa interview

Trip cancellation benefits only cover events after policy inception. Buying before submitting the visa application protects against refusal or delay.

BROKER TIP 2

Declare pre-existing conditions

Stable pre-existing conditions can be covered on premium tiers if declared. Failure to declare voids the entire medical section.

BROKER TIP 3

Consider annual multi-trip if you travel 3 plus times per year

AMT plans cap at 45 days per trip but cover unlimited trips. Cheaper than buying single trips for frequent travellers.

UAE Insurance Market Overview

An educational overview of the UAE insurance market structure, insurer types and the role of insurance intermediaries.

Conventional Insurance

Conventional insurance operates as a risk transfer contract. Policyholders pay premiums to an insurer who assumes the financial risk of covered events. UAE-licensed conventional insurers are regulated by the Central Bank of the UAE.

Takaful Insurance

Takaful is a Sharia-compliant cooperative insurance model where participants contribute to a shared fund. Surplus is distributed among members rather than retained as profit. Takaful operators in the UAE are regulated by the CBUAE.

Insurance Product Categories

UAE insurance products span motor, health, travel, property, marine, engineering, liability, energy, life and specialty lines. Products serve retail customers, SMEs and large corporate organisations.

Role of Insurance Brokers

Licensed insurance brokers act as independent intermediaries. They advise clients, compare products across multiple insurers, negotiate coverage terms, and assist with claims. Brokers must hold a valid CBUAE licence.

Digital Insurance Platforms

Digital insurance platforms like eSanad are licensed intermediaries that allow consumers to compare, purchase and manage insurance policies online. They work with multiple insurer partners to offer access to a wide product range.

Types of Insurers in the UAE

UAE insurers are classified as conventional, takaful, composite (offering both life and general products), or specialist (e.g., health-only). Most large UAE insurers are composite companies licensed to offer a broad range of products.

Best Classic Car Insurance Plans in UAE 2026

Classic car insurance is a specialized policy that protects vehicles registered as classic or vintage under UAE regulations.It provides coverage suited to cars that are preserved for exhibitions, leisure drives, or private collections — not everyday use.If your vehicle has been approved by the RTA (Dubai) or other emirate authorities as a classic, you’re required to maintain valid insurance for registration and renewal.Whether it’s a restored collectible or a rare model, the right insurance ensures your car is legally compliant and financially protected at all times.

Meets mandatory insurance requirements for classic vehicle registration and renewal.
Provides peace of mind when displaying or transporting your car for exhibitions or events.
Helps maintain your car’s insured value through agreed-value policies.

Why Do You Need Classic Car Insurance in the UAE?
Classic car insurance in the UAE is more than a requirement — it’s essential protection for your prized vehicle.
By law, every registered classic vehicle must be insured to remain compliant with UAE regulations. But beyond legal compliance, insurance safeguards your classic car’s value, craftsmanship, and legacy.
Accident Protection:

Covers damage or restoration costs if your classic car is involved in an accident — ensuring expert repair using approved workshops or restoration specialists.

Third-Party Liability

Meets UAE legal requirements by covering injury or property damage you may cause to others while driving your classic car.

Unexpected Risks:

Provides financial protection against risks like fire, theft, or transit-related damage — crucial for vehicles displayed, transported, or stored long-term.

Peace of Mind:

Enjoy full confidence knowing your registered classic car is insured, compliant, and protected, whether it’s on the road, at an exhibition, or safely stored.

Fines & Penalties in the UAE

Driving without valid insurance can lead to strict fines, penalties, and legal action in the UAE.

Driving Without Insurance

Fine: AED 500
4 Black Points on driving license
Vehicle impounded for up to 7 days

Fraudulent Insurance Claims

Hefty fines
Cancellation of policy
Possible legal action
Expired Insurance Policy
Renewal delays may lead to fines during traffic checks.
Vehicle may not be registered until valid insurance is provided.
Driving a Vehicle Not Covered by Insurance
If caught driving an uninsured or unregistered vehicle, additional penalties may apply, including extended impoundment.

Regional Enforcement

Abu Dhabi and Northern Emirates strictly monitor insurance compliance during renewals, traffic stops, and police checks.

Understanding your car insurance coverage helps you make informed decisions and avoid surprises during claims.

What Car Insurance Usually Covers?

Accidents, fire, and theft protection.

Natural disasters such as floods or storms.

Third-party liability for damages to others.

What Car Insurance Doesn't Cover?

Routine wear and tear or mechanical breakdowns.

Driving violations like driving without a license or under the influence.

Unapproved modifications or racing use.

Coverage outside the UAE, unless specifically included in your plan.

Best Electric Vehicle Insurance Plans in UAE 2026

Looking for smart, affordable, and eco-friendly electric vehicle (EV) insurance in Dubai, Abu Dhabi, or anywhere in the UAE?eSanad Insurance makes it easy to protect your EV with instant quotes, full coverage, and 100% RTAapproved policies — all in just a few clicks. Compare comprehensive and third-party EV insurance plans from trusted UAE insurers and get your policy issued online, instantly

Why Buy EV Insurance from eSanad?

EV insurance — also known as electric car insurance — provides financial protection tailored to electric vehicles on UAE roads.It covers damages caused by accidents, battery or charging system failures, fire, theft, and third-party liabilities.Since EVs use advanced technology, their repair and replacement costs can be higher than petrol or diesel vehicles — which is why specialized coverage is essential.In the UAE, having valid EV insurance is mandatory. Whether you drive a Tesla, BYD, MG, or any other electric brand, the right plan ensures you stay legally compliant and financially protected every time you drive

Why Do You Need EV Insurance in the UAE?
Protects against accidental damage, theft, and third-party liabilities.
Covers battery and charging equipment repairs or replacements.
Ensures peace of mind while driving your EV across the UAE.
Mandatory by UAE law for registration and RTA compliance.
Benefits & Reasons to Buy
Get instant policy issuance with eSanad’s 100% online process.
Access specialized coverage for EV-specific components.
Enjoy 24/7 customer support and transparent pricing.
Support the UAE’s sustainability goals while staying protected.
Why Do You Need EV Insurance in the UAE?
Car insurance in the UAE is more than a requirement, it's your legal and financial safeguard. By law, authorities require every vehicle to be insured to register or renew its license.
By law, all EVs must be insured for registration and renewal. But beyond compliance, EV insurance gives you peace of mind and specialized protection when you need it most.
Battery & Component Protection

Covers damage or replacement costs for EV batteries, charging systems, and essential electronic components.

Third-Party Liability

Ensures you’re covered if your EV causes injury to others or damages their property.

Unexpected Risks:

Provides coverage against fire, theft, natural disasters, and power surges affecting EV parts or charging stations.

Peace of Mind:

Drive confidently knowing your EV is fully protected and compliant with UAE laws — and that support is available 24/7.

With eSanad, you get more than just coverage — you get digital, transparent, and tailored EV insurance designed for the UAE’s sustainable future.

Before you start (quick checklist): Emirates ID, UAE driving license, Mulkiya (vehicle registration), previous policy/No-Claim Certificate (if any).

Select your plan

Upload your documents

Pay securely online

Receive your policy instantly

Fines & Penalties in the UAE

Driving without valid insurance can lead to strict fines, penalties, and legal action in the UAE.

Driving Without Insurance

Fine: AED 500
4 Black Points on driving license
Vehicle impounded for up to 7 days

Fraudulent Insurance Claims

Hefty fines
Cancellation of policy
Possible legal action
Expired Insurance Policy
Renewal delays may lead to fines during traffic checks.
Vehicle may not be registered until valid insurance is provided.
Driving a Vehicle Not Covered by Insurance
If caught driving an uninsured or unregistered vehicle, additional penalties may apply, including extended impoundment.

Regional Enforcement

Abu Dhabi and Northern Emirates strictly monitor insurance compliance during renewals, traffic stops, and police checks.

How to Get Chinese Car Insurance Online in UAE

Chinese car insurance — designed specifically for UAE residents driving Chinese-made vehicles — provides essential financial and legal protection on the road. Whether your car is damaged, stolen, or involved in an accident that harms others, your policy covers repair costs, medical expenses, and third-party liabilities.In the UAE, car insurance is mandatory for all drivers. eSanad helps you find coverage that fits your Chinese vehicle’s make and model — whether you drive Geely, MG, Changan, BYD, or Hongqi. From third-party liability to full comprehensive plans, our goal is to keep you compliant, protected, and confident every time you drive

Fines & Penalties in the UAE

Driving without valid insurance can lead to strict fines, penalties, and legal action in the UAE.

Driving Without Insurance

Fine: AED 500
4 Black Points on driving license
Vehicle impounded for up to 7 days

Fraudulent Insurance Claims

Hefty fines
Cancellation of policy
Possible legal action
Expired Insurance Policy
Renewal delays may lead to fines during traffic checks.
Vehicle may not be registered until valid insurance is provided.
Driving a Vehicle Not Covered by Insurance
If caught driving an uninsured or unregistered vehicle, additional penalties may apply, including extended impoundment.

Regional Enforcement

Abu Dhabi and Northern Emirates strictly monitor insurance compliance during renewals, traffic stops, and police checks.

What's Covered vs. Not Covered
Understanding your car insurance coverage helps you make informed decisions and avoid surprises during claims.

What Car Insurance Usually Covers?

Accidents, fire, and theft protection.

Natural disasters such as floods or storms.

Third-party liability for damages to others.

Medical expenses for drivers and passengers (depending on policy).

What Car Insurance Doesn't Cover?

Routine wear and tear or mechanical breakdowns.

Driving violations like driving without a license or under the influence.

Unapproved modifications or racing use.

Coverage outside the UAE, unless specifically included in your plan.

Why do you need Car Insurance in UAE?
Car insurance in the UAE is more than a requirement, it's your legal and financial safeguard. By law, authorities require every vehicle to be insured to register or renew its license.
But beyond compliance, car insurance protects you when you need it most
Accident Protection:

Covers repair costs or replacement of your vehicle.

Third-Party Liability

Ensures you're covered if you injure others or damage their property.

Unexpected Risks:

Fire, theft, and natural disasters are included in most comprehensive policies.

Peace of Mind:

Stay confident on the road, knowing you're fully protected and compliant.

Types of Car Insurance in UAE

Protection against accidents, fire, theft, and natural disasters.
Ideal for new or high-value cars.

Covers damages to other vehicles, property, or people.
Affordable option for budget-conscious drivers.

Classic Car Insurance

Tailored protection considering car value and limited usage.

Fines & Penalties in the UAE

Driving without valid insurance can lead to strict fines, penalties, and legal action in the UAE.

Driving Without Insurance

Fine: AED 500
4 Black Points on driving license
Vehicle impounded for up to 7 days

Fraudulent Insurance Claims

Hefty fines
Cancellation of policy
Possible legal action
Expired Insurance Policy
Renewal delays may lead to fines during traffic checks.
Vehicle may not be registered until valid insurance is provided.
Driving a Vehicle Not Covered by Insurance
If caught driving an uninsured or unregistered vehicle, additional penalties may apply, including extended impoundment.

Regional Enforcement

Abu Dhabi and Northern Emirates strictly monitor insurance compliance during renewals, traffic stops, and police checks.

What's Covered vs. Not Covered
Understanding your car insurance coverage helps you make informed decisions and avoid surprises during claims.

What Car Insurance Usually Covers?

Accidents, fire, and theft protection.

Natural disasters such as floods or storms.

Third-party liability for damages to others.

Medical expenses for drivers and passengers (depending on policy).

What Car Insurance Doesn't Cover?

Routine wear and tear or mechanical breakdowns.

Driving violations like driving without a license or under the influence.

Unapproved modifications or racing use.

Coverage outside the UAE, unless specifically included in your plan.

Musataha Insurance UAE 2026: Long-Lease Development Cover

Structured insurance programmes for 50-year Musataha developments in Abu Dhabi and across the UAE. Protecting landowners, developers, and financiers from construction through to reversion.

37Market Partners
ADMStandard Aligned
50 YearsLifecycle Ready

A Musataha agreement grants a developer the right to build on and operate land without owning it for up to 50 years. This unique legal structure, governed by Abu Dhabi Law 19 of 2005, requires specialized insurance that balances the interests of the landowner (often government entities like ADM), the developer, and the financier. eSanad structures tri-party programmes covering CAR, OAR, Loss of Rent, and Decennial Liability. Licensed by CBUAE (Licence 273) and DHA (BRK-0017).

Indicative Musataha Rate Snapshots
Cover Type	Villa Cluster	Logistics Park	Residential Tower	Hotel
CAR (Build Phase)	0.10%	0.14%	0.18%	0.22%
IAR (Operational)	0.10%	0.08%	0.12%	0.18%
Loss of Rent	0.08%	0.06%	0.10%	0.15%
Public Liability	AED 3,500/yr	AED 12,000/yr	AED 8,000/yr	AED 25,000/yr
Terrorism Loading	+10% to 18%	+10% to 15%	+12%	+15%

Disclaimer: Premiums indicative, subject to underwriting, project details, and T&Cs.

The Musataha Lifecycle & Insurance Needs
Phase 1: Construction
Contractor All Risk (CAR): Protects the asset during the build phase.
Delay in Start-Up (DSU): Protects anticipated rental income or debt service during build delays.
Performance Guarantees: Often required by ADM or the landowner.
Phase 2: Operation (The 50-Year Term)
Property/Industrial All Risk (IAR): Covers the completed structures against fire, storm, and natural perils.
Loss of Rent: Critical 24-36 month indemnity periods to reflect specialist Musataha re-letting reality.
Decennial Liability (Civil Code Art 880): 10-year structural warranty mandatory for towers and hotels.
Public Liability: Protecting the developer against third-party claims on the site.
Phase 3: Reversion or Renewal
Reinstatement Obligations: Covers the cost of required demolition or restoration at the end of the term.
Handover Condition Warranty: Insurance for the condition of the asset upon reversion to the landowner.
Why eSanad for Musataha Projects
ADM Alignment: Wordings strictly aligned with ADM Musataha Standard Contracts.
Tri-Party Programmes: Jointly naming Landowner, Developer, and Financier with appropriate Loss Payable clauses.
Sukuk Friendly: Experience in structuring programmes for Islamic finance and Sukuk investor security.
Long-Term Lifecycle: In-house management of renewals and claims across the full 50-year term.
Kehzad/Kizad Expertise: Specialist knowledge of logistics park Musataha requirements.
EEAT: Trust, Expertise & Practical Tips

Experience: Placements for KIZAD, Masdar City, and major Aldar-linked Musataha developments in Abu Dhabi.

Expertise: Technical coordination with ADM, Musataha legal counsel, and Sukuk trustees.

Broker Tips for Developers
Tip 1: Use 24-36 Month Loss of Rent. Musataha assets take longer to re-let than standard commercial units. Don't settle for the standard 12-month indemnity.
Tip 2: Name the Sukuk Trustee Early. Islamic finance loss payable clauses require specific wording. Draft these into the policy from day one to avoid payment delays.
Tip 3: Bundle Decennial Liability. DLI protects both developer and financier against structural defects for 10 years, sitting alongside your IAR policy.
Frequently Asked Questions
What is a Musataha agreement?

A long-term right (up to 50 years, renewable) to build on and use land without owning it, governed by Abu Dhabi Law 19 of 2005 and the UAE Civil Code.

How does Musataha insurance differ from standard property cover?

It balances three parties' interests (landowner, developer, financier) across a 50-year lifecycle and handles specific reversion and reinstatement obligations not found in standard PAR policies.

Is Decennial Liability required for Musataha?

Yes, for major assets like towers and hotels, a 10-year structural warranty (Decennial Liability) is typically required by lenders and authorities under Civil Code Art 880.

Does it cover land reversion?

Yes. Specialist reinstatement obligations cover can pay for required demolition or restoration to the agreed handover condition at the end of the lease term.

What happens if the developer defaults?

Mortgagee Interest Insurance (MII) and robust Loss Payable clauses protect the lender or sukuk holder's financial interest in the asset.

Questions About This Privacy Policy
Who We Are and What We Do

eSanad - Success Insurance Services L.L.C. (the “Company” or “eSanad”) is an insurance brokerage company providing brokerage services through conventional and digital platforms for customers to discover insurance quotations, compare them, and purchase motor, travel, home, health, and other insurance products according to their requirements.

The Company believes that the personal information of clients, prospects, partners, and website users should be respected and protected. This Privacy Policy explains how personal data is collected, used, processed, stored, disclosed, and protected when individuals interact with eSanad through its services, contact channels, website, and digital platforms.

In this Privacy Policy, “we”, “us”, and “our” refer to the Company and its successors, assigns, and permitted transferees. “You” and “your” refer to any person who uses our services, accesses our website, submits information to us, or otherwise communicates with us.

About This Privacy Policy

This Privacy Policy describes how the Company handles personal data relating to customers, prospective customers, website visitors, service providers, business contacts, regulatory counterparts, and other relevant individuals.

This Privacy Policy applies to interactions with eSanad through www.eSanad.com, associated digital platforms, contact center channels, forms, communications, and service touchpoints.

Third-party websites that may be accessed through our website are not covered by this Privacy Policy. eSanad is not responsible for the privacy or security practices of third-party websites, platforms, insurers, or service providers that operate under their own privacy terms.

How We Process Personal Data

We process personal data only where there is a valid legal basis and only for legitimate business, contractual, compliance, operational, service, support, fraud prevention, security, and communication purposes.

Processing may include collection, recording, organization, structuring, storage, adaptation, retrieval, consultation, use, disclosure, transfer, alignment, restriction, deletion, and destruction of personal data.

The categories of personal data processed and the way such data is used depend on the nature of the relationship with the individual, the services requested, the product involved, legal requirements, and the channels used to interact with us.

Categories of Personal Data We Collect

Depending on the circumstances, eSanad may collect and process the following categories of personal data:

• Identity information, such as name, nationality, date of birth, gender, Emirates ID, passport details, visa information, and other identification records.

• Contact information, such as mobile number, telephone number, email address, postal address, and communication details.

• Quotation and policy information, such as insurance product type, insured asset or risk details, policy preferences, coverage details, beneficiary information, prior insurance information, claim history, policy changes, renewals, and endorsements.

• Financial and payment information, such as billing details, transaction information, and payment-related records.

• Health or sensitive information, where relevant and lawful for underwriting, servicing, claims, or compliance in connection with health or related insurance products.

• Technical information, such as IP address, browser type, device identifiers, cookies, usage logs, and interaction data from our digital channels.

• Communication records, such as call recordings, chat transcripts, emails, complaint records, feedback, and service correspondence.

• Compliance information, such as due diligence records, fraud checks, sanctions screening information, declarations, and regulatory reporting information.

• CCTV and premises security information, where applicable for visits to physical premises.

Purposes of Processing

We may process personal data for one or more of the following purposes:

• To provide quotations, compare options, arrange insurance products, and facilitate policy issuance.

• To administer policies, renewals, endorsements, cancellations, claims support, and customer service requests.

• To verify identity, assess eligibility, prevent fraud, detect misuse, and maintain platform security.

• To communicate with customers and respond to enquiries, complaints, requests, or instructions.

• To comply with legal, regulatory, audit, risk management, and reporting obligations.

• To manage business operations, quality assurance, service improvement, analytics, training, and record keeping.

• To send service communications and, where permitted, relevant marketing communications.

• To protect the rights, property, safety, and legitimate interests of the Company, customers, insurers, and third parties.

Legal Basis for Processing

Depending on the circumstances, eSanad may process personal data on the basis of:

• Performance of a contract or taking steps prior to entering into a contract.

• Compliance with legal and regulatory obligations.

• Legitimate interests pursued by the Company or a relevant third party, where not overridden by data protection rights.

• Consent, where consent is required or appropriate.

• Protection of vital interests, where applicable under law.

Where sensitive personal data is processed, the Company will do so only where permitted by law and where an appropriate lawful basis applies.

Sources of Information

Personal data may be obtained from:

• The individual directly.

• Insurance proposal forms, applications, declarations, uploads, and communications.

• Insurance companies, underwriters, reinsurers, and claims administrators.

• Employers, sponsors, family members, or authorized representatives where appropriate.

• Government authorities, regulators, public records, verification agencies, and compliance screening providers.

• Cookies, tracking technologies, contact center systems, digital platforms, and Website analytics tools.

• Service providers and business partners assisting with technology, operations, communications, or support services.

Automated Processing

eSanad may use automated tools, rules-based processing, analytics, or profiling support in connection with quotation flows, fraud prevention, customer service workflows, digital platform operations, marketing preferences, and service optimization.

Automated processing may support efficient handling of requests, quality control, personalization, and risk management, but policy underwriting and final insurance decisions remain subject to the relevant insurer’s processes and approvals where applicable.

Disclosure of Information to Others

Personal data may be disclosed, shared, or made available on a need-to-know basis to:

• Insurance companies, reinsurers, and underwriting partners.

• Claims administrators, medical networks, garages, workshops, surveyors, and other service providers relevant to a product or claim.

• Payment processors, technology providers, cloud providers, customer support vendors, and communications providers.

• Professional advisers, auditors, legal counsel, and compliance consultants.

• Government bodies, regulators, courts, law enforcement agencies, and competent authorities where required or permitted by law.

• Group entities, affiliates, successors, assignees, or acquirers in connection with restructuring, transfer, merger, or business continuity arrangements.

The Company takes reasonable steps to ensure that recipients process personal data with appropriate confidentiality and security protections.

International Transfers and Limited Access Outside the UAE

Where operationally necessary and legally permitted, personal data may be accessed from or transferred to jurisdictions outside the UAE by service providers, technology partners, affiliates, insurers, or support teams involved in delivering services.

Where such access or transfer takes place, the Company will seek to implement appropriate safeguards and controls in accordance with applicable law.

How We Protect and Secure Data

The Company uses administrative, technical, physical, and organizational safeguards designed to protect personal data against unauthorized access, disclosure, alteration, misuse, destruction, or loss.

These measures may include access controls, authentication procedures, encryption, logging, monitoring, secure hosting arrangements, staff confidentiality obligations, training, and vendor oversight. No system can be guaranteed to be completely secure, but the Company works to maintain reasonable and appropriate protections.

How Long We Retain Personal Data

Personal data is retained only for as long as reasonably necessary for the purpose for which it was collected, including to provide services, maintain records, handle claims and disputes, satisfy regulatory obligations, enforce rights, conduct audits, and meet legal retention requirements.

Retention periods may vary according to the type of data, the nature of the relationship, the product involved, legal requirements, operational need, and limitation periods applicable to disputes or claims.

Your Rights

Subject to applicable law, individuals may have rights in relation to their personal data, including the right to:

• Request access to personal data.

• Request correction of inaccurate or incomplete data.

• Request deletion in certain circumstances.

• Request restriction of processing in certain circumstances.

• Object to certain processing activities where permitted by law.

• Withdraw consent where processing is based on consent, without affecting prior lawful processing.

• Request information about how personal data is processed.

• Lodge a complaint with a competent authority where applicable.

These rights are not absolute and may be limited where legal, regulatory, contractual, security, fraud prevention, or other legitimate grounds apply.

Marketing Communications

Where permitted by law, eSanad may send product, service, promotional, educational, or campaign-related communications. Individuals may opt out of non-essential marketing communications using the unsubscribe mechanism provided or by contacting the Company.

Service related, transactional, legal, compliance, or account communications may still be sent where necessary even if a marketing preference has been changed.

Modifications to This Privacy Policy

The Company may amend, update, or revise this Privacy Policy from time to time to reflect changes in legal requirements, services, technology, operations, or privacy practices.

Any updated version may be published on www.eSanad.com and will become effective from the date indicated in the revised version unless otherwise stated.

Applicable Law

This Privacy Policy is governed by the laws of the United Arab Emirates.

In the event of any conflict between this Privacy Policy and an applicable mandatory law, the applicable law shall prevail to the extent of the conflict.

Questions About This Privacy Policy

If you have questions, concerns, or requests relating to this Privacy Policy or the processing of personal data by eSanad, you may contact the Company using the contact details published on www.eSanad.com.

Terms and Conditions

eSanad - Success Insurance Services L.L.C. (the “Company” or “eSanad”) is an insurance brokerage company providing brokerage services through conventional and digital platforms for customers to discover insurance quotations, compare them and purchase insurance products and related services through the Company’s website at www.eSanad.com.

These Terms and Conditions govern the use of the Website and should be read together with the applicable terms and conditions of each insurance product issued by the relevant insurance company. By accessing, using, transacting through, or making any enquiry on the Website, the customer confirms that the customer has read, understood, and accepted these Terms and Conditions.

If any separate agreement or specific terms apply to a particular relationship, product, or service between the customer and the Company, those separate terms will prevail to the extent of any conflict.

Insurance products are offered by selected insurance companies and are subject to the approval, underwriting terms, policy conditions, exclusions, and acceptance criteria of the relevant insurer. Discounts and special offers may be made available by specific insurers and remain subject to eligibility, availability, selected product, and insurer approval.

The Website provides information and tools to help you find suitable insurance options from licensed UAE insurers. eSanad acts solely as a broker and does not endorse, guarantee, or take responsibility for the coverage, quality, claims handling, or performance of policies issued by insurers.

Definitions

Unless the context requires otherwise, the following definitions apply:

• Customer means any individual or entity accessing or using the Website, including any insured person applying for, enquiring about, or purchasing insurance products through the Website.

• Insurance Companies means insurance companies duly licensed and regulated in the UAE that provide insurance products marketed, introduced, or distributed through the Website.

• Insured Person means the person identified as insured under the applicable insurance policy issued by the relevant insurer.

• Insurance Policy means the policy document and related wording issued by the relevant insurer setting out the applicable terms, conditions, exclusions, endorsements, and benefits.

• Insurance Products means the insurance products made available through the Website by insurance companies.

• Service Provider means any third-party provider assisting the Company in providing services through the Website.

• UAE means the United Arab Emirates.

• Website means the Company’s website located at www.eSanad.com and any associated digital platform operated by the Company.

• Personal Data means any information relating to an identified or identifiable natural person.

Insurance Products and Services

The Company acts as an insurance broker and facilitator between customers and insurance companies. Insurance quotations, policy issuance, renewals, endorsements, and related services made available through the Website are provided subject to insurer participation and approval.

The Company may offer access to a broad range of insurance services, including products for motor, travel, home, health, and other personal or commercial insurance lines, together with digital support tools and related service features made available from time to time.

Any quotation displayed on the Website is indicative unless expressly confirmed by the insurer. Final premium, coverage, underwriting acceptance, exclusions, excess, benefits, and policy issuance remain subject to insurer review and approval.

The Company may also facilitate digital claims support, service requests, endorsements, cancellations, policy enquiries, loyalty or promotional features, and contact center support. Availability of any feature may vary by insurer, product, geography, and operational requirements.

Customer Undertakings and Responsibilities

By using the Website, the customer undertakes and agrees:

• To provide true, accurate, current, and complete information.

• To promptly update any information previously submitted if it changes.

• Not to impersonate any person or entity or misrepresent identity, authority, or affiliation.

• To ensure that all information submitted for quotation, underwriting, issuance, claims, or service requests is complete and not misleading.

• To review all quotation details, policy schedules, terms, exclusions, and endorsements before purchase.

• To maintain the confidentiality of account credentials, verification codes, and access details.

• To use the Website only for lawful purposes and in compliance with all applicable laws and regulations.

The customer acknowledges that inaccurate, incomplete, outdated, or misleading information may lead to rejected quotations, revised premiums, delayed services, claim denial, voidance of coverage, cancellation, regulatory reporting, or insurer refusal.

The customer remains solely responsible for ensuring that selected insurance products are appropriate for the customer’s needs and for reviewing all policy documentation issued by the insurer.

Privacy Notice

The Company collects, uses, stores, and discloses personal data in accordance with its Privacy Policy published by eSanad from time to time. By using the Website and submitting information, the customer acknowledges and agrees that the Company may process personal data as necessary for quotation, broking, servicing, compliance, fraud prevention, communications, analytics, customer support, and related legitimate business purposes.

Where required by law, the Company will rely on consent or another valid legal basis for processing personal data. The customer confirms that any personal data submitted about another person has been lawfully obtained and shared with appropriate authority.

Offer, Acceptance, and Termination

Information, content, and quotations on the Website do not necessarily constitute a binding offer by the Company or by any insurer. A policy is only concluded when the relevant insurer accepts the proposal, receives any required documentation and payment, and issues the policy or confirms cover.

The Company may decline, suspend, or terminate access to the Website or any service at any time where reasonably necessary for security, compliance, technical, operational, legal, or misuse-related reasons.

The customer may stop using the Website at any time. Termination of Website access does not affect rights, obligations, liabilities, or policy terms already accrued or already in force.

Purchase of Insurance Policy

To purchase an insurance policy through the Website, the customer may be required to submit personal, policy, risk, and payment information, upload supporting documents, complete declarations, and accept the applicable insurer’s policy wording and terms.

The customer acknowledges and agrees that:

• Policy issuance is subject to insurer approval.

• Coverage, benefits, exclusions, and conditions are determined by the insurer and policy wording.

• Payment confirmation alone does not guarantee policy issuance unless accepted by the insurer.

• The customer must verify all issued policy details immediately upon receipt and notify the Company or insurer of any discrepancy.

• Cancellation, refund, endorsement, and claims handling are subject to the insurer’s applicable terms, regulatory requirements, and operational processes.

Electronic records, confirmations, declarations, clicks, checkboxes, OTP verification, and digital communications may be used as evidence of instructions, consent, acceptance, and transaction authorization.

Modification and Access

The Company may modify, suspend, withdraw, restrict, or discontinue any part of the Website, content, functionality, or services at any time without prior notice, where permitted by law.

The Company does not guarantee uninterrupted or error-free access to the Website and may perform maintenance, upgrades, security updates, or system changes from time from time.

Intellectual Property Rights

All intellectual property rights in and to the Website, including software, text, graphics, design, layout, trademarks, logos, service marks, databases, compilations, audio, video, content, and underlying technology, are owned by or licensed to the Company unless otherwise stated.

The customer is granted a limited, non-exclusive, non-transferable, revocable right to access and use the Website for personal or internal business use only in accordance with these Terms and Conditions.

The customer shall not, without prior written consent of the Company:

• Copy, reproduce, republish, upload, post, transmit, or distribute Website content.

• Modify, adapt, translate, reverse engineer, decompile, disassemble, or create derivative works.

• Remove proprietary notices or branding.

• Use the Company’s name, logo, marks, or content in any misleading or unauthorized manner.

Third-Party Sites

The Website may contain links to third-party websites, insurer portals, payment gateways, tools, or external platforms. These are provided for convenience only.

The Company does not control and is not responsible for the content, availability, security, privacy practices, terms, or services of any third-party site. Accessing any third-party site is at the customer’s own risk, and the customer should review the relevant third-party terms and privacy notices before using them.

Cookies

The Website may use cookies, analytics tools, pixels, and similar technologies to support functionality, security, traffic analysis, personalization, service improvement, and marketing measurement.

By continuing to use the Website, the customer acknowledges such use subject to applicable law and the Company’s Privacy Policy. Browser settings may allow some cookie preferences to be managed, though disabling certain cookies may affect Website functionality.

Indemnity and Limitation of Liability

To the fullest extent permitted by law, the customer agrees to indemnify and hold harmless the Company, its affiliates, officers, employees, representatives, agents, and service providers from and against any claims, losses, liabilities, damages, costs, and expenses arising out of or related to:

• Breach of these Terms and Conditions.

• Misuse of the Website.

• Violation of applicable law or third-party rights.

• False, inaccurate, incomplete, or misleading information provided by the customer.

• Unauthorized use of credentials or account access attributable to the customer.

To the fullest extent permitted by law, the Company shall not be liable for any indirect, incidental, special, consequential, exemplary, or punitive loss or damage, including loss of profits, business, opportunity, goodwill, data, or anticipated savings.

The Company shall not be liable for delays, interruptions, failures, or loss arising from events beyond its reasonable control, including insurer decisions, system outages, connectivity failures, cyber incidents, force majeure events, governmental actions, or third-party service disruptions.

Nothing in these Terms and Conditions excludes or limits liability where such exclusion or limitation is prohibited by law.

Miscellaneous Provisions

• If any provision of these Terms and Conditions is held invalid or unenforceable, the remaining provisions will continue in full force and effect.

• No waiver by the Company of any breach or right shall be deemed a continuing waiver.

• These Terms and Conditions may be assigned by the Company to any successor, affiliate, or transferee.

• No agency, partnership, joint venture, employment, or franchise relationship is created by these Terms and Conditions.

• Section headings are for convenience only and do not affect interpretation.

Governing Law and Jurisdiction

These Terms and Conditions and any dispute, claim, or matter arising out of or in connection with them, the Website, or any related dealings shall be governed by the applicable laws of the United Arab Emirates.

Subject to any mandatory legal or regulatory provisions, the courts of the United Arab Emirates shall have jurisdiction to hear and determine disputes arising out of or in connection with these Terms and Conditions.

Contact Details

For questions regarding these Terms and Conditions, the customer may contact eSanad using the contact details published on www.eSanad.com.

Key Takeaways
What You Need to Know About Motor Claims

The essential facts before you file your motor insurance claim in the UAE.

24-Hour Rule

You must notify your insurer within 24 hours of the accident. Delays can result in claim rejection.

Police Report Is Mandatory

UAE law requires a police report for all accidents. Call 999 before moving any vehicles involved.

3–10 Days Processing

Standard motor claims are processed within 3–10 working days. Complex cases may take longer.

Agency vs. Non-Agency Repairs

Agency repair policies allow authorized manufacturer garages. Check your policy for repair entitlements.

No-Claim Discount at Risk

Filing a claim may affect your no-claim discount (NCD) at renewal. Consider the excess before claiming.

Third-Party Claims Differ

If you're claiming against another driver, you need their insurer details. Esanad can help you navigate this.

Step-by-Step Guide
How to File a Motor Claim: 6 Simple Steps

01

Report the Accident

Call 999 immediately to report the accident and obtain a police report. This is mandatory for all motor insurance claims in the UAE.

02

Gather Your Documents

Collect your Emirates ID, driving license, Mulkiya (vehicle registration), police report, and photos of all vehicle damage.

03

Notify Your Insurer

Contact your insurance provider within 24 hours of the accident. Delay may affect your claim eligibility.

04

Submit Your Claim

File your motor claim online through esanad.com or visit a service center. Include all required documents.

05

Vehicle Assessment

An insurance surveyor will inspect your vehicle and assess the repair costs within 1–2 working days.

06

Claim Settlement

Once approved, your vehicle is sent for repair at an approved garage, or you receive your settlement payout.

Documents Checklist

Frequently Asked Questions

Q
1

How do I file a motor insurance claim in the UAE?
Contact your insurer within 24 hours of the accident. Get a police report (call 999), gather your Emirates ID, driving license, Mulkiya, and damage photos. Submit your claim via esanad.com or at a service center.

​
Complaint Handling Policy
Complaint Handling Policy
Complaint Log:
The customer can log the complaint by following means:
By call to our complaint helpline no 600 500 888
By email to info@esanad.com
By online chat bot
By welcome you in our office at M02, Mezzanine Floor, Al Barakah Tower, Al Najda Street, Al Danah, Abu Dhabi.
The Customer Care Executive logs the complaint as per customer discussions with above means and get to know their rights in First Information Report.
The Customer Care Executive request customers to submit documents (if Any).
After the communication, the customer care executive send Complaint report to customer for acknowledgement in 48 hours.
Complaint Report:
After Logging the complaint, the customer care executive prepares a complaint report with all points discussed as per complaint report with customer.
The complaint report has been authorized by line Manager.
Once the Line Manager authorized, the complaint is sent to customer via email from info@esanad.com and request for acknowledgement in 48 hours.
If there is no reply from customer, the complaint report sent to customer is acknowledged. Once acknowledged by customer, the customer care executive sent complaint report to insurance representative in eSanad for handling customer complaint with insurance company.
Investigation with Insurance Company:
The Insurance representative (IR) explain the customer complaint with insurance company (INS).
The IR represents customers rights with the existing policy information after submitting all documents to the insurance company.
The IR will follow up with insurance company and request INS to provide solution to customer for their complaint.
After the completion of investigation stage, eSanad will contact the customer to inform him/her about results and propose suitable actions.
The customer agrees the solution and acknowledged. The complaint is closed and send for feedback stage.
The duration of this stage is 7-8 working days.
Escalation: 1 Compliance Officer:
If Customer not agrees the solution, the complaint raised escalation to Compliance Officer.
The compliance officer evaluates the customer rights after reviewing complaint report and communicate with customers. The compliance officer understands customer’s point of view and the root cause of issue.
The Compliance Officer communicate with insurance company for customer requirement and workout the best possible solution for customer.
The customer agrees the solution and acknowledged. The complaint is closed and send for customer feedback stage.
The duration of this stage is 1-2 working days.
Escalation:2 Management Committee:
If Customer not agrees the solution, the complaint raised further escalation to Management committee (MC).
The Compliance Officer explains the complaint to MC and customer requirement.
The MC agrees for favorable solution and written report submitted to client for acknowledge the proposed solutions.
The customer agrees the solution and acknowledged. The complaint is closed and send for customer feedback stage.
The duration of this stage is 4-5 working days (Put up in earliest MC meeting).
Request Client to complain DHA/DOH/CBUAE:
The company puts all its efforts to satisfy customer with their rights and entitlements. But if customer will not agree the best possible solutions, the company provides information to escalate to DHA for Dubai & DOH for Abu Dhabi and other emirates. The customer can complain to respective authority by following means.
For Dubai Health Insurance complaints, you can contact the Dubai Health Authority (DHA) using the online link https://www.isahd.ae/Home/Ipromes or the details below:
E-Mail: cg@dha.gov.ae
Toll Free (24/7): 800342 (800 DHA)
For Abu Dhabi Health Insurance complaints, you can contact the Health Authority of Abu Dhabi (HAAD) using the online link https://www.doh.gov.ae/ or the details below:
Email: contact@abudhabi.ae
Telephone: +971 2449 3333 or Local Toll-Free Number: 800 555.
For all other UAE Insurance Policies, you can contact the Central Bank of the UAE using the online link https://smartservices.ia.gov.ae/ecomplaint/ecomplaint/complaint or the details below:
Email: contactus@ia.gov.ae
Telephone: 800 22823 (800 CBUAE)
The duration of this stage is 1 working day (Put up in earliest MC meeting).
Complaint Register:
The company records all complaint in complaint register within 2 working days.
The complaint register is maintained digital as well as hard copy.
The complaint register needs to be present to CEO desk in every 6 months and approved by CEO.

Cookies Notice
Cookies Notice
1. eSanad Cookies Notice


This Cookies Notice describes how eSanad – Success Insurance Services L.L.C. ("eSanad", "we", "us", or "our") uses cookies and similar tracking technologies across our website and online platforms. It also explains the purposes for which these technologies are deployed and outlines the choices available to you in managing their use.

We use cookies to ensure the website functions properly, enhance user experience, analyse performance, improve our services, and—where permitted—deliver relevant advertising.

For comprehensive information about how eSanad processes and protects Personal Data more broadly, including your rights under applicable UAE data protection laws, please refer to our Privacy Notice.

2. Definition of Cookies


Cookies are small text files placed on your browser or device when you visit a website. They are widely used to store, retrieve, and transmit identifiers and other information across computers, mobile phones, tablets, and similar devices. These files help enable core website functions and improve the efficiency, security, and personalization of your online experience.

In addition to traditional cookies, we may use similar tracking technologies that function in comparable ways, including:

browser or device-based storage technologies (such as local storage and session storage);
unique device identifiers;
tracking pixels and pixel tags;
web beacons;
scripts, tags, and similar technologies used for analytics, security, and functionality.

For the purposes of this Cookies Notice, all such technologies—including those listed above—are collectively referred to as "cookies".

3. Personal Data Collected via Cookies


Cookies generally contain anonymous unique identifiers that interact with your browser when you visit our website. When you access the site for the first time, our systems send data to your browser, which stores it in a cookie file. During subsequent visits, your browser returns this information to our servers, enabling us to recognise your device and improve your browsing experience.

Through this process, cookies may collect certain categories of Personal Data by accessing information stored on your browser or device. Depending on the cookie type and your settings, this may include:

Identifiers
user IDs
device IDs
session IDs
advertising identifiers (where applicable)
Device Information
IP address
browser type and version
device type and model
operating system and configuration
network and connection details
Geographic Data
language preferences
regional content optimisation
fraud detection and security
Behavioural and Usage Data
pages viewed and navigation paths
buttons or links clicked
time spent on each page
scrolling behaviour
website performance data and error logs

This data is used to support core website functionality, enhance user experience, improve site performance, secure our digital environment, and enable analytics or advertising activities where legally permitted.

4. Types of Cookies We Use


We use several categories of cookies to ensure the smooth operation, security, and optimisation of our website and digital services. These cookies enable essential site functions, enhance performance, support personalization, facilitate analytics, and—where legally permitted—help deliver relevant marketing content.

The specific cookies in use may vary depending on the pages you visit, the features you interact with, and your cookie preferences.

Below is an overview of the primary types of cookies we use:

1. Strictly Necessary Cookies

These cookies are essential for the functioning of our website and cannot be disabled through our internal cookie settings. They are required to:

enable secure login and authentication,
support site navigation,
facilitate the use of essential features, such as form submissions,
maintain system stability and protect against fraudulent activity.

Without these cookies, core elements of the website may not work properly. These cookies do not store personally identifiable information.

2. Performance / Analytics Cookies

These cookies help us understand how visitors use our website by collecting aggregated, anonymous analytics data. They allow us to:

measure site performance,
identify popular and less-visited pages,
analyse user navigation patterns,
diagnose technical issues and improve loading speed.

The insights gained help us continuously improve website functionality and user experience. You may disable these cookies, but some performance features may be affected.

3. Functional Cookies

Functional cookies enable enhanced features and personalization, including:

remembering your settings and preferences,
retaining customisations made during your session,
enabling interactive tools or embedded content.

These cookies may be set either by eSanad or by trusted third-party providers whose tools are embedded into our website. Disabling functional cookies may impact your browsing experience.

4. Targeting / Advertising Cookies

These cookies may be placed by our advertising or marketing partners. They help us:

understand your interests based on browsing behaviour,
deliver relevant advertisements on our website or third-party platforms,
measure campaign effectiveness,
support remarketing activities.

These cookies typically do not store direct Personal Data. Instead, they rely on unique browser or device identifiers. If you disable targeting cookies, you may still see ads online, but they will be less tailored to your interests.

5. Cookies Placed by Third Parties


When using our website, applications, or digital services, you may encounter cookies that are placed by third-party service providers rather than eSanad. These third-party cookies support both essential and non-essential functions such as analytics, performance optimisation, advertising delivery, and content integration.

eSanad may partner with carefully selected third parties who use cookies to:

analyse and understand how you browse and interact with our website;
display relevant information, promotions, and service offerings while you are using our site;
deliver personalised advertisements on third-party websites, apps, or social media platforms based on your browsing behaviour and interests (remarketing);
measure the reach and effectiveness of advertising and marketing campaigns;
support performance monitoring and technical diagnostics across our website and digital platforms;

When third-party cookies are active, the information collected may be processed by those providers according to their own privacy and cookies policies. We encourage you to review the privacy notices of any applicable third parties to understand how they handle your data.

Where required under UAE data protection laws or international standards, we will request your prior consent before enabling third-party cookies, particularly those used for analytics, targeting, or advertising.

You may disable or manage third-party cookies at any time through your browser’s cookie settings or by adjusting your cookie preferences on our website. Please note that restricting certain third-party cookies may affect the functionality or personalization available on our site.

6. List of Some Cookies Used on Our Website


Below is an overview of some of the cookies used on our website, including details about their category, purpose, source, and duration.

7. Controlling and Opting Out of Cookies


You have multiple options to manage, limit, or disable cookies on your device. These controls allow you to customise your browsing experience and regulate how your information is collected through cookies and similar technologies.

Using Private or “Incognito” Mode

Most modern browsers offer a private or “incognito” browsing mode. When using this mode:

cookies and site data are not stored after you close the browser window;
browsing history and form data are not saved;
only essential, temporary cookies required for basic functionality may operate during your session.

Using private mode helps limit the collection of certain cookies and reduces the amount of browsing information stored on your device.

Managing Cookies Through Your Browser

Most web browsers allow you to manage cookies directly. Depending on your browser, you may be able to:

block new cookies from being placed on your device;
receive alerts when a website attempts to store a cookie;
delete cookies already stored on your browser;
disable specific categories of cookies while allowing essential ones;
clear browsing data, including cached files, stored website preferences, and saved form entries.

To modify your cookie preferences, navigate to the “Settings,” “Preferences,” or “Privacy & Security” section of your browser. You may also consult your browser’s “Help” menu for detailed guidance.

Additional Browser-Specific Guidance

For step-by-step instructions, you may refer to external resources for:

Google Chrome
Microsoft Edge
Apple Safari
Mozilla Firefox
Opera
Microsoft Internet Explorer

These links are provided for your convenience only. They are operated and maintained by the respective browser providers, and eSanad is not responsible for their content, accuracy, or availability.

For broader information and browser-specific cookie management guidance, you may also visit:
www.allaboutcookies.org

Please note that if you choose to block, disable, or delete certain categories of cookies, some features of our website or online services may not function as intended. This may affect website performance, certain interactive features, and your overall browsing experience.

8. Contact


If you have any questions, requests, or concerns regarding this Cookies Notice, or if you wish to exercise any rights related to the use of cookies or similar technologies, you may contact us at:

Email: hello@esanad.com

We will review your enquiry and respond in accordance with applicable UAE data protection laws and regulatory requirements.

9. Effective Date and Changes to This Notice


This Cookies Notice is effective as of 19 March 2025.

We may update or amend this Notice from time to time to reflect changes in our operational practices, legal or regulatory requirements, or enhancements to our technology and services. Any revisions will be published on this page, and the updated version will become effective immediately upon posting.

We encourage you to review this Notice periodically to stay informed about how we use cookies and similar technologies and how these practices may evolve over time.

What to Do After a Car Accident in UAE
1
Stop safely and turn on hazard lights.
2
Ensure all parties are safe — call 999 if anyone is injured.
3
Call the police and obtain an accident report number.
4
Do not move the vehicles until instructed by police.
5
Exchange details with the other driver (name, licence, insurance policy).
6
Take photos of both vehicles and the scene.
7
Contact your insurer or eSanad to initiate the claims process.
8
Take your vehicle to an insurer-approved garage for assessment.

What to Consider Before Choosing This Insurer

Neutral guidance to help you evaluate whether Arabia Insurance is the right fit for your insurance needs.

Coverage Limits & Exclusions

Review policy exclusions carefully. Common exclusions include pre-existing conditions, wear and tear, and certain high-risk activities. Ensure coverage limits match your needs.

Medical Network Strength

Check whether your preferred hospitals and clinics are within the insurer's approved network. A broader network means fewer out-of-pocket expenses.

Claims Process Expectations

Understand how claims are submitted, what documentation is required, and the typical processing time. A straightforward claims process can make a significant difference.

Garage Network Availability

For motor insurance, verify that approved garages are conveniently located for you. Ask whether agency repairs are included in your policy.

TPA Availability

Confirm which TPA administers your medical claims. The quality and responsiveness of the TPA directly affects your day-to-day experience with medical coverage.

Premium vs Coverage Balance

The lowest premium is not always the best value. Compare coverage scope, excess amounts and sub-limits before deciding based on price alone.

Policy Documentation Clarity

Read the policy wording thoroughly. Clear, straightforward policy documents are a sign of a transparent insurer. Seek clarification on anything ambiguous before purchasing.

What to Do After a Car Accident in UAE
1
Stop safely and turn on hazard lights.
2
Ensure all parties are safe — call 999 if anyone is injured.
3
Call the police and obtain an accident report number.
4
Do not move the vehicles until instructed by police.
5
Exchange details with the other driver (name, licence, insurance policy).
6
Take photos of both vehicles and the scene.
7
Contact your insurer or eSanad to initiate the claims process.
8
Take your vehicle to an insurer-approved garage for assessment.

What to Consider Before Choosing This Insurer

Neutral guidance to help you evaluate whether RAK INSURANCE is the right fit for your insurance needs.

Coverage Limits & Exclusions

Review policy exclusions carefully. Common exclusions include pre-existing conditions, wear and tear, and certain high-risk activities. Ensure coverage limits match your needs.

Medical Network Strength

Check whether your preferred hospitals and clinics are within the insurer's approved network. A broader network means fewer out-of-pocket expenses.

Claims Process Expectations

Understand how claims are submitted, what documentation is required, and the typical processing time. A straightforward claims process can make a significant difference.

Garage Network Availability

For motor insurance, verify that approved garages are conveniently located for you. Ask whether agency repairs are included in your policy.

TPA Availability

Confirm which TPA administers your medical claims. The quality and responsiveness of the TPA directly affects your day-to-day experience with medical coverage.

Premium vs Coverage Balance

The lowest premium is not always the best value. Compare coverage scope, excess amounts and sub-limits before deciding based on price alone.

Policy Documentation Clarity

Read the policy wording thoroughly. Clear, straightforward policy documents are a sign of a transparent insurer. Seek clarification on anything ambiguous before purchasing.

Celebrating eSanad's Milestones and Industry Recognitions

Since its inception, eSanad has been at the forefront of the insurance technology (insurtech) revolution in the UAE. Our news and events section is dedicated to sharing our journey, from winning prestigious industry awards to participating in global fintech and insurance forums. We are committed to transparency and providing our stakeholders with the latest updates on our growth and community engagement.

Award-Winning Insurtech Excellence

eSanad has been recognized by various industry bodies for its innovation in simplifying the insurance buying process. Our platform leverages advanced data analytics and artificial intelligence to provide users with the most accurate motor, health, and travel insurance quotes. These accolades reflect our team's hard work and our dedication to customer-centricity.

Events and Community Engagement

We believe in the power of knowledge and regularly participate in events like GITEX Technology Week and the Insurtech Insights summits. These platforms allow us to collaborate with leading insurers like Orient, Sukoon, and RAK Insurance to develop better products for the UAE market. Beyond technology, we are also active in local community events, promoting road safety awareness and digital financial literacy across Dubai, Abu Dhabi, and the Northern Emirates.

Pay Your Insurance in 6 Easy Monthly Installments — 0% Interest

Through our strategic partnership with Ubey Credit Card, issued by Mawarid Finance PJSC, eligible customers can choose to pay their insurance premiums in six (6) equal monthly installments at 0% interest.

No added premium.
No hidden mark-ups.
No impact on your insurance coverage.

Just smarter cash flow management.

Whether you are buying Motor, Medical, or others, you can now spread your payment over six months subject to credit approval and card eligibility.

Check your eligibility
How It Works
Select your insurance policy through eSanad.
Choose Ubey Credit Card as your payment method.
If you already hold a Ubey card — you can proceed immediately.
If you don’t — apply directly through Ubey (https://ubeyy.com/esanad).
Upon approval, pay your premium using the Ubey card and activate the 6-month Easy Payment Plan (EPP).

Your insurance policy is issued as usual by the insurer. Your installments are handled directly by Ubey Finance.

Regulatory & Structural Disclosure - Important Information
eSanad is a licensed insurance intermediary regulated by the Central Bank of the UAE.
The 6-month Easy Payment Plan (EPP) is offered exclusively by Mawarid Finance PJSC through the Ubey Credit Card.
Credit approval, limits, eligibility, and installment terms are determined solely by Mawarid Finance.
eSanad does not provide lending, financing, credit underwriting, or installment services.
Insurance issuance, pricing, and underwriting remain independent of any financing arrangement.
Offer Terms & Conditions
Easy Payment Plan (EPP) – Terms & Conditions

1.

The 6-month 0% Easy Payment Plan (“EPP”) is offered exclusively by Mawarid Finance PJSC through the Ubey Credit Card and is subject to Mawarid Finance’s approval, internal credit policies, and applicable cardholder terms and conditions.

2.

Eligibility for the EPP is subject to:

Holding a valid and active Ubey Credit Card; and/or
Meeting Mawarid Finance’s credit approval criteria.

3.

Credit approval, credit limits, installment conversion, repayment schedules, and all related financing obligations are determined solely by Mawarid Finance and are subject to its regulatory and contractual requirements.

4.

eSanad acts solely as a licensed insurance intermediary regulated by the Central Bank of the UAE and does not:

Provide lending, financing, or credit services;
Conduct credit assessments or affordability evaluations;
Influence approval decisions;
Collect installment payments;
Process or retain credit-related documentation; or
Assume any credit, default, collection, or repayment risk.

5.

Any late payment charges, fees, penalties, or applicable interest (if any under the cardholder agreement) shall be governed exclusively by the Ubey Credit Card terms and conditions issued by Mawarid Finance.

6.

The availability of the EPP does not affect:

Insurance underwriting decisions;
Premium pricing;
Policy coverage terms;
Claims handling processes; or
The insurer’s contractual obligations.

7.

Insurance policies are issued by duly licensed insurance companies in the United Arab Emirates and remain subject to the insurer’s underwriting criteria, policy terms, conditions, and exclusions.

8.

The Easy Payment Plan is an independent financing arrangement between the cardholder and Mawarid Finance. It does not form part of the insurance contract and does not modify, enhance, or replace any insurance policy terms.

9.

eSanad reserves the right to amend, suspend, or withdraw the display of this offer on its website at any time, subject to applicable regulatory requirements.

10.

Mawarid Finance reserves the right to modify, suspend, or discontinue the Easy Payment Plan in accordance with applicable laws, regulatory guidelines, and its internal policies.

11.

Any dispute, inquiry, or complaint relating to credit approval, installment payments, fees, card usage, or financing obligations must be directed to Mawarid Finance PJSC.
Additional Salik Promotional Benefit – Terms & Conditions
Customers who are successfully issued and activate a Ubey Credit Card under this campaign may receive a promotional AED 100 Salik top-up.
The Salik benefit is provided, funded, and administered solely by Mawarid Finance PJSC and is subject to Mawarid Finance’s promotional terms and conditions.
The Salik top-up is a promotional benefit and does not constitute an insurance discount, premium rebate, or underwriting incentive. It does not affect insurance pricing, coverage, or policy terms.
eSanad does not fund, administer, process, or credit the Salik top-up and bears no responsibility in relation to its issuance or usage.
The Salik credit is non-transferable, non-refundable, and not redeemable for cash.
To qualify, the customer must maintain a valid and active Salik account registered in their own name at the time of crediting.

How the Fine Is Calculated

Understand the rules for each emirate before your fine keeps growing

🏙️

Dubai Fine Rules

›

AED 500 per person per month (even 1 day = full month)

›

Grace period: 30 days after policy expiry

›

Maximum fine: AED 150,000

›

Visa renewal blocked if unpaid

›

Law: Dubai Health Insurance Law No. 11 of 2013

🏙️

Abu Dhabi Fine Rules

›

AED 300 per person per month

›

Applies to all residents and dependents

›

TAMM portal used to check and pay fines

›

Visa/residency renewal at risk

›

Authority: Department of Health (DoH) Abu Dhabi

AE

Other Emirates (From Jan 2025)

›

Federal mandate covers: Sharjah, Ajman, RAK, Fujairah, UAQ

›

AED 500/month federal rate applies

›

Required before visa issuance or renewal

›

Law: UAE Cabinet Health Insurance Scheme 2025

Who Is Responsible?

Understanding liability for health insurance in the UAE

👔
Employers
Must insure all employees. Fine: AED 500/month per uninsured worker
👨‍👩‍👧
Visa Sponsors
Must insure spouse, children, and domestic workers under their sponsorship
🏠
Domestic Workers
Maids, drivers, and nannies must be covered by their visa sponsor
🏢
Companies
Repeat violations can result in AED 500,000 fine and business license issues
Frequently Asked Questions

Q
1

How much is the fine for not having health insurance in Dubai?
AED 500 per person per month. Fines can reach AED 150,000 for severe or repeated violations under Dubai Law No. 11 of 2013. Even a single day without coverage in a given month counts as a full month's fine.

🏛️ ABU DHABI OFFICIAL PORTAL
TAMM — Abu Dhabi Government Services

TAMM is the official Abu Dhabi government portal where residents can check, manage, and pay health insurance-related fines and obligations.

AE
TAMM Portal

Abu Dhabi's unified digital government platform

Visit tamm.abudhabi →
What You Can Do on TAMM

Check outstanding health insurance fines linked to your Emirates ID

Pay fines online using credit/debit card or UAE Pass

Download Certificate of Continuity (COC) for visa renewal

Link and manage insurance policies for your dependents

View your complete insurance compliance history

Access DoH Abu Dhabi health insurance services

How to Access Your Fine
1
Visit tamm.abudhabi

Go to the official TAMM portal on your browser or smartphone

2
Log in with UAE Pass

Use your UAE Pass app, or sign in with Emirates ID + password

3
Search Health Insurance

Navigate to Health Insurance Services under the DoH Abu Dhabi section

4
Check & Pay Fine

Enter your Emirates ID to view outstanding fines and pay securely online

LOGIN METHOD

UAE Pass or Emirates ID

KEY DOCUMENT

Certificate of Continuity (COC)

AUTHORITY

Department of Health Abu Dhabi (DoH)
